bullets.text: France has confirmed its first case of Ebola in a doctor who had been working in the Democratic Republic of Congo, where over 260 people have died from the disease.
The UN nuclear chief announced that inspectors will visit Iranian sites as part of a war deal, though an Iranian minister stated such access would only occur as part of a final agreement with the US.
Colombia's left-wing presidential candidate has conceded defeat to a right-wing businessman, who won by a narrow margin of less than a percentage point.
Teal independent MPs Zali Steggall and Allegra Spender have launched a new political party called Community Strong Australia, aimed at the political centre.
Former Liberal Party vice-president Teena McQueen, who served from 2017 to 2023, has died after a short illness.
The Australian Security Intelligence Organisation (ASIO) director-general has expressed concern about the "unprecedented" terrorism threats currently facing Australia.

Venezuela is expected to reveal a $240 billion debt pile, significantly more than anticipated, as it prepares for the world's largest debt restructuring following the overthrow of the Maduro government.
All 32 of the largest US banks successfully passed the Federal Reserve's annual stress tests, enabling them to immediately increase share buybacks and dividends.
Hedge funds' increased exposure to US Treasuries is largely attributed to the resurgence of the cash-futures basis trade, according to a Federal Reserve economist.
